description: In a bedroom filled with open cardboard boxes and piles of clothing, Monica stands near the bed while Rachel faces her. Phoebe sits on the bed holding a glass. Monica gestures toward Rachel and says, "Ever. Hey, I call her."
Monica turns her head away while Rachel keeps her gaze on her. Audience laughter is audible.
Monica stands in front of a floral painting, clutching her hands to her chest and looking upward. Her volume is loud and her pitch high as she says, "Oh, my God. I love Ross."
Monica moves her hands up and down rapidly, maintaining her high volume as she says, "I hate Ross. I love Ross."
Monica stops her hand movements and lowers her volume slightly as she says, "I hate Ross."
Monica looks to her left. Audience laughter continues.

Rachel, holding a black garment, looks toward Monica. With a high volume and pitch that mimics Monica's previous lines, she says, "Oh, my God. I can't find a boyfriend. So I guess I'll just stumble across the hall and sleep with the first guy I find in there."
Monica crosses her arms and watches Rachel. Rachel walks past Monica. Audience laughter is audible.
Phoebe sits on the edge of the bed, holding a glass of dark liquid and looking toward Rachel and Monica.
Phoebe looks between the two women and says, "Come on, this is the last night."
Rachel turns back toward Phoebe and responds, "Yeah, you're right. You're right."
Rachel puts her hands on her hips and looks at Monica. She says, "And so for the last time, I would like to ask you boohoo. Get out of my room." Her voice on "boohoo" uses a mocked crying quality.
Monica keeps her hands on her hips and watches Rachel. Audience laughter is heard.
Monica picks up a large plastic container filled with blue rolls from the top of a cardboard box.
Phoebe stands up from the bed. Cardboard boxes are scattered around the room.
Monica walks out of the bedroom. Phoebe begins folding a piece of clothing and says, "Yeah, we should get a move on if we want to make those dinner reservations."
Rachel remains in the bedroom, looking toward the doorway Monica just exited.
Now outside on a city street at night, a red-fronted building stands on a corner. A street sign reads "Grove St." Instrumental transition music plays.
Now inside another apartment, Chandler stands with his arms crossed over a foosball table. Joey stands near several stacked cardboard boxes in the background.
Joey walks toward Chandler and the foosball table, saying, "Still broken."
Chandler looks down at the table players.
Joey looks down at the table and says, "Sucks, man."
Joey gestures with his left hand as he says, "Your last night here, and I lose the two most important things in my life."
Joey gestures toward the table and says, "the foosball table and $500."
Chandler looks at Joey, his arms remaining crossed.
Chandler steps closer to Joey and says, "But there are other ways you can win back your money."
Chandler mimes dealing cards and asks, "How about a little blackjack?" Joey shakes his head and responds, "Not my game."
Chandler gestures with one hand and says, "Okay, how about you know what? We could play a new game. A new game? It's"
Chandler continues gesturing as he says, "fun." Joey looks at him and asks, "Well, what's it called?"
Chandler looks around briefly and then looks at Joey, saying, "Cups."
Joey looks back at Chandler. Audience laughter is audible.
Joey says, "I don't know how to play Cups." Chandler gestures for Joey to follow him toward the kitchen counter, saying, "I'll teach it. Come on. Come on. It's really easy."
Chandler stands behind the counter and says, "Really, really fun. All right. Okay, here you go." He begins dealing playing cards onto the counter.
Chandler places cards in front of Joey. Joey looks down at the counter.
Chandler looks at his own cards and says, "I have two queens. What do you have?"
Joey looks at his cards and says, "A two and a five." Chandler responds, "You win $50."
Joey looks up at Chandler with his mouth slightly open. Audience laughter is heard.
Joey asks, "Really?"
Chandler picks up the cards and says, "Oh, yeah. Okay, let's play again."
Chandler asks, "Ready?"
Chandler deals cards onto the counter with fast hand movements. Joey watches closely.
Joey looks at his cards and asks, "What do you got?" Chandler looks at his and says, "Four and a nine. You're kidding, right?"
Joey keeps his gaze on his cards.
Chandler holds his cards up and says, "No. Why? Well, that's a full cup."
Joey looks at the cards in Chandler's hand. Audience laughter is audible.
Joey looks down at the counter and says, "Damn, I am good at cups."
Chandler begins picking up the cards again.

Now in the previous apartment's living room, Monica sits on a coffee table near a large cardboard box. She says, "I'm not talking to her." Phoebe stands nearby and says, "One of you has to take the first step."
Phoebe gestures with her hand and says, "And it should be you because she's the one who's leaving. It's harder for"
Phoebe finishes her line: "her."
Monica looks down at the floor.
Monica stands up and says, "Maybe you're right." She walks toward a closed door. "But she made fun of my phone pin."
Phoebe fiddles with a comb and says, "I know. I took it hard too."
Monica stops in front of the door and turns back toward Phoebe.
Phoebe gestures toward the door and says, "She's in there right now crying. I can hear her."
Monica turns to the door and leans her ear against it.
Monica looks back at Phoebe and says, "She's not crying." Phoebe responds, "She's holding it in. She's so brave."
Phoebe points to the door and says, "Go,"
Monica opens the door and walks into the bedroom.
Monica walks toward Rachel, who is throwing clothes into a box, and says, "Rachel. Feel really bad about what happened." Rachel asks, "What are you doing?" and then says, "I'm unpacking."
Monica's mouth stays open as she says, "What?" Rachel turns toward a wall and says, "I'm not moving. Is that picture straight?"
Rachel adjusts a picture frame on the wall. Monica gestures toward her and says, "It needs to go about 20 blocks to the left."
Rachel turns away from the wall to face Monica.
Rachel points a finger at Monica and says, "Hey, you know what? You're the one who wants to make this big change and move in with Chandler. You should be the one to go."
Monica gestures around the room and asks, "Why do I have to leave?" Rachel responds, "Because it's my apartment." Monica says, "What's?" and Rachel says, "Mine too. What else you got?"
Monica stands still, looking at Rachel.
Monica shouts, "How about you're moving!"
Monica reaches for a pile of clothes. Rachel says, "What? No."
Monica and Rachel both grab handfuls of clothes and shove them into a large cardboard box. Both women repeatedly say "No!" and "Yes!" loudly as they struggle over the laundry.
Rachel stops and holds her hands out toward Monica, saying, "Okay, Monica,"
Both women stand still, breathing heavily and looking at each other.
Rachel gestures with both hands and says, "this is ridiculous."
Monica looks down at the box of clothes.
Rachel reaches for more clothes and says, "We should be packing. You."
Rachel leans over the box of clothes.
In Monica's apartment, cardboard boxes are scattered across the living room and kitchen. Rachel runs through the living room toward the kitchen, carrying an open, empty box.

Rachel continues running into the kitchen and asks, "Hey. How you guys doing?" Phoebe stands in the kitchen holding papers and scissors, while Monica enters through the blue doors in the background. Audible audience laughter begins.
Phoebe turns her head slightly toward Monica and says, "Great. Monica's moving." Monica stops behind Phoebe.
Monica stands still with her mouth open and her eyebrows raised.

Monica says, "I am not," as she steps further into the kitchen. Rachel bends over a box on the floor, and Monica says, "Oh, really? Well, how come all of your stuff is in this?" while picking up an object from a box.
Rachel runs quickly across the kitchen floor with a box. Monica follows her as they move in a loop around the kitchen table.

Phoebe raises both hands and says, "You guys, I think I know what's going on here. Okay, you guys, stop." Monica and Rachel continue running around the kitchen furniture. Audible audience laughter continues.
Monica and Rachel both stop moving and stand on opposite sides of the kitchen.

Phoebe gestures with both hands as she says, "I know that I know that you're acting mad because you think that'll make it easier to leave." Monica stands by the table while Rachel stands by the kitchen counter.
Phoebe continues gesturing and says, "But deep down, you're still really sad. Deep, deep down."
Monica looks toward Phoebe and says, "No, Phoebe, I am mad." Phoebe's hands remain raised.
Phoebe says, "Well, deep, deep, deep down."
Rachel, now holding a bowl filled with vegetables, looks at Phoebe and says, "Yeah, I'm just mad."
Phoebe looks between Monica and Rachel.
Phoebe makes a small motion with her right hand and says, "Then keep running."
Monica and Rachel resume running around the kitchen table in opposite directions. Audible audience laughter is present. The setting changes to Chandler and Joey's kitchen.

Chandler and Joey sit at their kitchen counter with cards and cash spread out before them. Chandler holds several cards and says, "You win. What did you have? Well, it doesn't matter. Nothing beats a three and a six."
Chandler places cards down on the counter and says, "That, my friend, is decup." Joey looks at his cards and then at the cash on the counter.
Joey's gaze remains on the money and cards on the counter.
Chandler leans forward and asks, "Okay, now, how much have you won so far?"
Joey picks up a stack of bills and begins counting them.
Joey counts the stack of cash and says, "Wow, 700." Chandler watches him count. Audible audience laughter.
Chandler says, "Not 700 exactly," while looking at the money in Joey's hands.
Joey looks up from the cash to face Chandler.
Chandler flicks his right hand and says, "Yeah, double it."
Joey says, "What?" his eyebrows rising and his eyes widening.
Chandler leans closer to Joey across the counter.
Chandler gestures with his hands and says, "Yes. In Cups, once you get $700, you have to double it."
Joey continues holding the stack of money, his gaze fixed on Chandler.
Chandler points to himself and says, "Hey, I didn't make up the rules." Audible audience laughter.
Chandler reaches for a card on the counter.
Chandler holds up a single playing card and says, "Okay? So now after you receive the doubling bonus, you get one card."
Chandler looks at the card in his hand and says, "Now, that one card could be worth $100, which brings your total to 1500."
Joey leans in toward the card Chandler is holding.
Chandler holds the card face down and says, "Don't get too excited, because that's not going to happen unless you get the no way."
Chandler flips the card over. Joey's eyes widen and his mouth drops open. The setting changes back to Monica’s living room.
Monica carries a large box from the living room toward the kitchen. She says, "Well, this is the last box of your clothes. I'm just going to label it. What were you thinking?"
Monica places the box down on the floor. Rachel stands in the kitchen holding the bowl of vegetables.
Rachel gestures with a piece of broccoli and says, "Funny, because I was just going to go across the hall and write that on Chandler." Monica stands beside her in the kitchen.
Monica remains by the counter. Phoebe enters the kitchen from the living room.
Phoebe stops in the center of the kitchen and says, "Okay, you guys, I don't mean to make things worse, but I don't want to live with Rachel anymore."
Monica looks at Phoebe and asks, "What?"
Phoebe gestures with her hands as she says, "You're just so mean to each other. And I don't want to end up like that with Rachel. I still like you." Monica and Rachel remain still.
Rachel turns and says, "Well, Phoebe, that's fine because I'm not moving."
Monica steps toward Phoebe with her hands outstretched and says, "Whoa, whoa, whoa whoa whoa. Phoebe, you got to take her." Rachel stands in the background.
Monica turns her body to face Phoebe.
Monica clasps her hands together and says, "I know I said some really bad stuff about her but Rachel does have some good qualities that make her a good roommate."
Rachel, sitting at the kitchen table, asks, "Oh, really? Like what?"
Rachel looks at Monica and adds, "Monica." Monica looks back at Rachel.
Monica pauses and looks upward, her mouth slightly open.
Monica gestures with both hands and says, "You know, she has 147,000 pair of boots?"
Rachel, still at the table, responds, "Oh, yeah. Good start, Mon." Audible audience laughter.
Monica turns back toward Phoebe.
Monica leans toward Phoebe and says, "She lets you borrow them."
Rachel looks at Monica.
Rachel crosses her arms over her chest and says, "Yeah, and you stretch them out with your big old clown feet." Audible audience laughter is audible.
Monica turns to face Rachel again.
Monica points her finger toward the hallway and says, "Do you want to live outside?"
Monica continues, "Because it's getting cold." Rachel keeps her arms crossed.
Monica turns back to face Phoebe.
Monica says, "She gets tons of catalogs and she'll fold down the pages of the things that she thinks that I'd like."
Phoebe, standing with her arms crossed, asks, "What else?"
Monica looks around the room.
Monica says, "When I take a shower, she leaves me little notes on the mirror."
Rachel, still at the table, says, "Yeah, I do. I do do that. That's" while fiddling with an object.
Phoebe says, "nice. I like having things to read in the bathroom." Phoebe's arms remain crossed.
Monica turns and walks toward a box on the coffee table in the living room.
Monica stands by the couch and says, "When I fall asleep on the couch after reading, she covers me over with a blanket."
Rachel says, "Well, you"
Rachel looks down at the table and says, "know, I don't want you to be cold."
Monica stands still beside the box, her gaze on Rachel.
Monica looks at Phoebe and says, "And when I told her that I was going to be moving in with Chandler"
Monica stops speaking and blinks several times.
Monica says, "she was really supportive," her volume softer than her previous lines. Audible audience laughter.
Phoebe looks at Monica. Monica remains standing by the box. Segment ends.
In the kitchen area, Monica stands beside Rachel, who is seated at a wooden table.
Monica leans over and places a hand on Rachel's arm. "You were so great," she says. Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel looks up at Monica.
Monica holds Rachel's hands. "You made it so easy," she says. Audience laughter continues.
Monica looks down at their joined hands.
Monica pulls her hands away and clutches her chest. "And now you have to leave. And now you have to live with a boy," she says, her voice breaking on the final phrase. Audience laughter is audible.

Monica turns and runs toward the refrigerator, placing her hands over her face while making loud sobbing sounds. Rachel stands and follows her, placing a hand on Monica's shoulder. Audience laughter continues.
Rachel embraces Monica and says, "It's gonna be fine," her volume soft and voice slightly trembling. Audience laughter is audible.
In the background, Phoebe stands with her arms crossed, watching them.

Phoebe walks toward them and says, "Okay, Rachel. Now I can't wait to live with you. And you know what we should do? Bring Monica. And then we can all live there," her volume rising on the final phrase.
Phoebe continues, "together. We'll have so much fun," her volume rising on "so much fun." Monica and Rachel remain in an embrace. Audience laughter is audible.
Monica and Rachel look toward Phoebe, still holding each other.
Rachel says, "But, honey, I think she's moving in with Chandler." Phoebe responds, "Oh, that's right." Audience laughter is audible.
Phoebe gestures with one hand and asks, "You're still set on that?"
Monica nods while her head is still resting on Rachel's shoulder.
"Kinda," Monica says. Audience laughter is audible.
Monica and Phoebe embrace briefly before Monica walks out of the kitchen area.
Now in a bedroom, Monica stands by a window and says, "What?"
Through the window, Ross is visible in the apartment across the way, sitting on a couch.
Monica says, "Oh, my God," her volume loud.
Inside Ross's living room, Ross sits on a couch eating popcorn from a blue bowl. A phone rings.
Ross reaches to his right, picks up a cordless phone, and pulls the antenna.
Ross says, "Hello? Hey, mom."
Ross continues into the phone, "How's the packing going? Ben?"
Ross says, "He's fine. Yeah, he's right oh, my God." He turns his head to the left toward a striped sweater stuffed with an object. Audience laughter is audible.
Ross sets the popcorn bowl on the coffee table and reaches for the stuffed sweater.
Ross says, "Get your head out of your shirt there, son," as he pulls at the sweater's neck. Audience laughter is audible.
Ross pulls a large pumpkin out of the neck of the sweater.
Ross says, "What?" into the phone. Audience laughter continues.
Ross says, "Yeah, it's a pumpkin. I'll come pack." He lowers the phone. Audience laughter is audible.

Outside, the city street is dark. Upbeat instrumental music with a drum beat plays. The setting changes to a living room with cardboard boxes. Chandler stands over a box while Joey enters through a door.
Chandler looks toward Joey and asks, "Hey, what's wrong?" The music stops.
Joey stands with his hands on his hips and says, "Ross and I were helping the girls pack. Took a little break."
Joey continues, "I lost one $500 to him at Cups," and raises his hands. Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler looks at Joey, holding a small object in his right hand.
Chandler asks, "How did you lose at Cups?" his volume louder than his previous line.
Joey walks further into the room and says, "The same way you lost. I start out with the king and a queen. Bam. Ross gets a two and a three." Audience laughter is audible.
Joey continues walking toward the kitchen counter.
Joey says, "Then I get a jack and a king. Boom. Ross, it's a four and a five," gesturing broadly with both arms. Audience laughter is audible.
Joey reaches the counter and leans against it.
Joey says, "Ross was getting the cup card, the D Cup, the sitting down bonus," his pitch rising on "D Cup." Audience laughter is audible.
Joey says, "Meanwhile, I didn't even get a half a cup. Nothing," then sits in a brown leather recliner. Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler stands with his arms crossed, watching Joey.
Joey says, "Oh, man. He never played before. Really? You know what I think? I think beginners lock very important in Cups."
Chandler begins to walk toward Joey.
Chandler says, "Let's just play one more hand. Just one more hand." Joey responds, "No, no, no more. I cannot lose another dime. I'm serious this time. In fact," while raising one hand.
Joey stands up and says, "look, I want to give you something, okay? And let me give it to you now before I pawn it for Cups money," then walks toward the hallway.
Chandler remains near the counter as Joey rolls a large white ceramic dog statue on a platform into the room.
Joey says, "Now, I want you to have the big white dog as a kind of like a thank you for being such a great," and places his hands on the statue's back.
Joey finishes, "roommate." Chandler says, "I can't take the big white dog. You love it." Joey responds, "It's him, not it." Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler looks at the dog statue and then back to Joey.
Chandler asks, "What if I bought it from you? And then your nice gesture would be giving it to me at a reasonable price, say, $1,500?" Audience laughter is audible.
Joey looks at the dog statue, then points a finger at Chandler.
Joey says, "Wait a second. I see what you're trying to do here." Audience laughter is audible.
Joey steps closer to Chandler.
Joey says, "You're trying to give me money again," his volume louder than his previous line.
Chandler raises his eyebrows and looks at Joey.

Chandler asks, "When did I try to give you money?" Joey says, "Before, over there with the bills. You tried to give me charity? I said no. You," and gestures toward the kitchen counter. Audience laughter is audible.
Joey continues, "dropped it. Okay, then we had a nice last night together."
Joey says, "We had some fun. We gambled. Nobody tried to give anybody any money," and spreads his arms.
Chandler remains still, leaning one hand on the dog statue.
Joey asks, "Now, out of the blue, you'd start with the charity thing again?"

Chandler says, "I'm just trying to help you out, okay? I want to make sure that you're okay." Joey responds, "I will be okay. Look, Chandler, you got to get it out of your," speaking with his hands raised.
Joey says, "head that I can't take care of myself, okay?" and leans slightly toward Chandler.
Joey says, "Look, I'm not going to miss you helping me out with money."
Joey remains still, his gaze fixed on Chandler.
Joey says, "The only thing that I'm going to miss is you," his volume softer than his previous lines.
Joey and Chandler stand facing each other in silence. Audience laughter is audible as the segment ends.
In a living room, Joey and Chandler stand near a large white dog statue. Joey looks at Chandler while Chandler keeps his gaze fixed forward.
Joey shifts his gaze to the statue. "And now the dog," he says. Chandler watches him without moving.
Chandler remains silent, his gaze fixed on Joey. Audience laughter is audible.
The setting changes to an outdoor view of a city skyline across a body of water during sunset. Acoustic guitar music plays.
Inside an apartment, Ross and Joey carry a small wooden table through an open doorway. Monica, Rachel, and Phoebe stand near the door. Monica places her hands on Rachel's arms.
Monica looks at Rachel and says, "me when you get there. Okay?" her volume low. Phoebe stands behind Monica.
Rachel says, "Okay. Really gonna miss you," her voice slightly trembling.
Rachel leans forward and hugs Monica, resting her head on Monica's shoulder. Monica wraps her arms around Rachel. Soft sobbing and audience reaction are audible.
The two women remain in the embrace. Rachel's hands are pressed against Monica's back.
Rachel pulls back and wipes her eyes. "Oh, yeah. I have your key," she says, reaching into the shoulder bag she is wearing.
Rachel searches through her bag as Monica watches her.
Rachel pulls a set of keys from her bag and holds them toward Monica. "There you go," she says.
Monica's hand moves forward to meet Rachel's.
Monica takes the keys and says, "Thanks."
Rachel nods slightly and says, "Yeah."
The two women stand facing each other in silence. Rachel's eyes are wet and she blinks frequently while Monica looks at her.
Rachel steps forward again and says, "Oh, God. This is silly. I'm going to see you in a couple of hours," her volume rising. She hugs Monica again.
Still hugging Monica, Rachel looks around the room and says, "It it. Bye, house." Monica rests her head against Rachel's.
They remain in the hug. Monica draws a sharp breath and laughs slightly.
Rachel releases the hug and turns toward the open doorway, adjusting the bag on her shoulder.
"Bye," Rachel says as she walks out. "Bye," Monica responds, her hand raised slightly.
Monica turns back toward the room, her gaze moving across the apartment. Phoebe stands nearby.
Monica walks toward the door, her movements slow. Acoustic guitar music continues.
Monica reaches for the door handle and slowly closes the door. She looks at the empty coat hooks on the wall.
The setting changes to a different room with pink walls and a tan carpet. Monica walks through a doorway and stops, looking into the empty room.
Monica leans her shoulder against the doorframe, her gaze fixed forward. Her breathing is audible.
Chandler walks up behind Monica and places his right hand on her shoulder. Monica does not turn her head.
Monica says, "She really left," her volume lower than her previous lines.
Chandler stands close behind her and says, "I know."
Chandler wraps both arms around Monica from behind. Monica leans back into him and crosses her arms over his.
Monica says, "Thank you." Chandler responds, "No problem. roomie," his voice muffled as he rests his chin on her shoulder.
They remain in the embrace, swaying slightly from side to side. Acoustic guitar music continues.
Monica blinks and looks toward the doorway. Chandler keeps his eyes closed while leaning on her.
Monica turns her head slightly toward Chandler and asks, "can I ask you a question?" "Sure," Chandler says.
Monica looks toward the living room and asks, "What the hell is that dog doing here?" The white dog statue is visible in the distance.
Chandler and Monica continue to look toward the statue. Audience laughter is audible.
The setting changes to a living room with a brown leather sofa. Chandler walks across the room while Ross stands near the sofa holding a bottle.
Chandler says, "I invented the game of Cups as a way to give Joey money." Ross looks toward him.
Ross says, "And now you want that money back." "Exactly," Chandler replies as he sits on the sofa.
Ross gestures with his hands and asks, "Chandler. What kind of idiot do you take me for?" his volume louder than Chandler's.
Ross picks up a striped sweater from the sofa and walks toward the kitchen.
From the sofa, Chandler says, "It's not a real game. I made it up."
Ross turns back from the kitchen doorway and says, "I'm sorry you lost your money. But I've won it fair and square at a fake game."
Chandler sits back on the sofa, his gaze fixed on Ross.
Ross walks back toward the sofa and says, "Now, if you want to try to make some of it back, I'd be glad to play you for it."
Ross says, "But I should warn you, I am," as he reaches into his pocket.
Ross pulls a deck of cards from his pocket and sits on the sofa next to Chandler.
Ross looks at Chandler and says, "very good at Cups."
Chandler takes several cards and says, "Okay? Now, I assume the saucer card came up when you played last," his gaze on the cards.
Ross looks at his own cards and says, "No."
Chandler pulls a card from his hand and says, "Let's see if it comes up this time."
Chandler places a card on the coffee table. Ross leans forward to look at it.
Chandler raises his hands and says, "Team." Ross stares at the table. Audience laughter is audible.
The scene begins to fade to black as white text credits appear on the screen.
Instrumental music continues over the black screen with credits until the segment ends.
